title: Koeleria cheesemanii
intro: Koeleria cheesemanii, also known as Cheeseman's hair grass, is a species of perennial grass native to New Zealand. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its various health benefits, including the treatment of fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. The bioactive constituents of Koeleria cheesemanii include phenolic compounds, flavonoids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Koeleria cheesemanii include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to promote overall well-being and support the immune system.
taxonomy: Koeleria cheesemanii is a species of grass that belongs to the family Poaceae. It is a member of the subfamily Pooideae and the tribe Poeae. The genus Koeleria is a group of perennial grasses that are commonly found in temperate and alpine regions. Koeleria cheesemanii is native to New Zealand and is characterized by its bright green leaves and delicate flower spikes. As a member of the Poaceae family, Koeleria cheesemanii is closely related to other grasses such as wheat and oats.
